attributes.content:
## Overview

The Incident card gives the workspace a structured home for production incident: severity, timeline, root cause, resolution, and postmortem.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Incident is designed to participate in active workflows. Its state, relationships, and outputs need to remain inspectable so that agents and humans can collaborate on the same object without losing context.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Integration]]** -- Incident can sync with or depend on external systems through the shared integration layer.
- **[[Trigger]]** -- Changes to Incident can fire automation, and automation can also create or update Incident.
- **[[Task]]** -- Incident often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Project]]** -- Incident can be grouped under a larger initiative so its work stays visible in context.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Incident can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Tool Definition]]** -- Tool calls can read from or write to Incident when automation needs structured side effects.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Incident as the structured home for production incident: severity, timeline, root cause, resolution, and postmortem and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Incident when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Incident stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.
